sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: initial allocation = MAX{(-largest_historical_loss / -optimal_f), (margin + ABS(max_drawdown))}; remaining equity divided by optimal f in dollars to determine additional contracts. Example: optimal_f=0.4, max_drawdown=$6,000

source_rule_text: Allocate initial contract size based on the maximum of either the largest historical loss divided by optimal f, or margin plus absolute maximum drawdown; then calculate additional contracts by dividing remaining equity by optimal f in dollars and rounding down to integer.
